caption:
REX (Rakan Exco) is part of the SRC family, playing an active role in supporting SRC initiatives throughout the year, from organising events and executing programmes to enhancing campus life.

But REX goes beyond volunteering.
It’s a platform to develop your leadership potential, strengthen your event management skills, boost your confidence, and form meaningful connections. Along the way, you’ll gain hands-on experience, explore new opportunities, and be part of a community that shapes your university journey. 🌱✨

💥 Interested in making an impact?
Apply now via the QR code or link below:
📌 https://forms.office.com/r/EtzpCqcLpP

🗓️ Deadline: 10th May 2026

Have questions? Reach out anytime:
📞 Natasha (REX Manager): +6012-6073706

Let’s learn, contribute, and grow together.
#REXRecruitment #REX2026 #RakanExco #ThereIsYouInMMU #MMUCyberjaya

Dear Students,

Have you been experiencing issues with Outlook, EBWISE, or the CLiC system? 🖥️
We hear you! SRC has received feedback from students regarding various technical problems including:
🔴 Login difficulties
🔴 Delayed email access
🔴 System errors
🔴 Other technical disruptions

We want to make sure your concerns are heard and escalated to the right departments! 💪
📋 Please fill in our report form so we can:
✅ Understand the issues you're facing
✅ Gather supporting evidence
✅ Escalate to the university's IT & relevant departments
✅ Work towards resolving these issues ASAP

🔗 Tap the link below to report your issue:
https://forms.office.com/Pages/ResponsePage.aspx?id=z18LfsQS_06WtkZk8l3H2rGTsa2BPAJGl-rNl5-p0rVUOENUTUZQQjJNQU5QRFJSNUc2QVpRN0Q2MC4u

📎 You may also attach screenshots or provide additional details in the form to support your report.

All information collected will be used strictly for documentation and escalation purposes. Your feedback matters! 🙏

Best Regards,
Students' Representative Council, Student Academic & Entrepreneurship Division 2025/2026

Dear Students,

We are Students' Academic and Entrepreneurship Division (SAED), under the Students' Representative Council (SRC) term 2025/2026.

This survey is being conducted to gather students' feedback regarding the proposed arrangement for 25th, 26th, 28th, and 29th May 2026, that inform students during each classes will be conducted as an ONLINE WEEK.

Please take note of the following holidays during this period:
27th May (Wed): Eidul Adha
31st May (Sun): Wesak Day
1st Jun (Mon): YDP Agong's Birthday
2nd Jun (Tue): Substitute Public Holiday (Wesak Day)

The purpose of this survey is to better understand students' opinions and preferences. Collected responses will be compiled and presented to the relevant departments for further discussion and consideration.

For further inquiries, please feel free to contact: 
MATTHEW SIMON Executive Officer
Students' Academic and Entrepreneurship Division (SAED) Students' Representative Council 2025/2026

Multimedia University, CyberjayaTEL: +6017-306-640

Regards,
Students' Representative Council, Students' Academic and Entrepreneurship Division 25/26

SRC MMU successfully conducted the Be-Xpert Workshop 2026 in collaboration with STyLE, delivering a meaningful and engaging session for students ✊✨

The workshop provided a valuable platform for learning and knowledge sharing, covering key areas such as club management, event planning, proposal writing, and financial procedures. Participants also gained insightful guidance from the STyLE team through interactive and informative sessions.

We sincerely appreciate the time, effort, and dedication of STyLE, as well as the participation from all attendees in making this workshop a success. We look forward to more impactful initiatives in the future 🚀🌟

#BeXpert2026 #MMU #SRC #STyLE #StudentDevelopment

Happy Labour Day! ⚙️✨

Dear MMU Community,
Today, we celebrate all the amazing individuals who make our MMU community so vibrant and strong. To our dedicated lecturers, hardworking campus staff, and driven students. Thank you for your continuous hard work, creativity, and the unique energy you bring to campus every single day.
Whether you're teaching in the lecture halls, working behind the scenes to keep the university running, or studying for your future, we appreciate you. Wishing you a well-deserved break and a wonderful Labour Day! 💪

Access to basic hygiene should never be a struggle.

Recently, the Student Welfare Division of SRC in collaboration with Blood Sanitary Pads, distributed over 1200 packs to both students and staff. 

Thank you for everyone that dropped by, supported, participated and interacted with us. We hope to continue pushing out more initiative like this that focus on student welfare and everyday needs. 

Best Regards,
Students' Representative Council 2025/2026

We hear you, and this happened because of YOU.

Over 500+ of you took the time to respond to our survey for the SRC 4 Siswa Food Security Initiative, and every single response made a difference. Because of your voices, your concerns, and your support, we were able to push this forward and have meaningful discussions with the management.

Alhamdulillah, we’re happy to share that management is now on board with the initiative. This is what happens when students come together.

Thank you for being part of this.

Best regards,
Students’ Representative Council 2025/2026

Dear Students,

Please be informed that the last date to enrol in courses for this trimester is Thursday, 30 April 2026.

Students who encounter difficulties enrolling due to outstanding fees or a finance bar must meet with the Finance Officer for consultation and to appeal for unbarring no later than 28 April (Tuesday) 2026.

Please contact: Finance Office 
Email: finance@mmu.edu.my 
Phone: Cyberjaya: 03-8312 5135 / 03-8312 5473 / 03-8312 5141 
Melaka: 06-252 3267 / 06-252 3452 / 06-252 3471

Kindly note the following important points:
1.No late registration will be entertained after the deadline. 
2.Students who fail to enrol by the deadline are required to apply for a Leave of Absence (LOA). 
3.No appeal for course registration / enrolment after 30 April 2026 will be considered.

Your cooperation in adhering to these deadlines is greatly appreciated. Thank you.

Best Regards,
Students' Representative Council, Student Academic & Entrepreneurship Division 2025/2026

✨ Nominations for MMU Awards 2025 are now OPEN! ✨

The MMU Awards celebrates the dedication, talent, and achievements of students, clubs, and programmes that continue to make a meaningful impact within the Multimedia University community.

Do you know someone who consistently goes the extra mile? Or a programme or club that has created a positive impact on campus? This is your opportunity to recognise those who inspire, lead, and contribute to making MMU a vibrant and thriving community.

📝 How to nominate?
Visit our page and check out the latest posts for:
• The full nomination timeline
• A step-by-step nomination guide
• The QR code / link to submit your nomination

‼️Disclaimer 
Each club may nominate a maximum of 2 programme categories. Any submissions beyond this limit will not be considered.

Let’s come together to celebrate the individuals and initiatives that make our community stronger.

📞 For enquiries, please contact:
Mawar Jazmina Atiyah binti M.Jeffridin (Head of Communication & Operations) – 017-675 6640
Alkabbani Nawaf Shafik M (Asst. Head of Communication & Operations) – 011-6153 9766

✨ Nominate today and be part of recognising excellence at MMU Awards 2025.

Good luck to all nominees! 🏆

MMU SRC President Darren John Led 100 Students in Developing a Resolution Supporting Lonjakan: Keunggulan Global Under the Rancangan Pendidikan Tinggi Malaysia (RPTM) at the Makmal & Dialog Kemenjadian Mahasiswa

The session brought together diverse student perspectives to align with Malaysia’s broader goals for global competitiveness in higher education. Under his coordination, the discussion focused on strengthening initiatives that support international exposure, academic standards, and graduate readiness.

The resulting resolution was formally presented to Zambry Abd Kadir, Minister of Higher Education @zambryabdkadir , and Adam Adli Abd Halim, Deputy Minister of Higher Education @adamadli_ , along with senior officials from the Ministry of Higher Education (KPT) @mohe.official . This reflects MMU’s constructive engagement in national-level policy discussions.

This initiative highlights MMU’s continued participation in advancing global aspirations within higher education, while demonstrating collaborative contributions from GLU institutions in supporting Malaysia’s long-term objectives.

Through structured leadership and representation, President Darren John continues to ensure MMU’s voice is present in meaningful conversations shaping the future of higher education in Malaysia.

caption:
MMU SRC proudly participated in the Makmal & Dialog Kemenjadian Mahasiswa 🇲🇾

This historic national platform gathered 981 student leaders from Public Universities (IPTA), Private Higher Education Institutions (IPTS), Polytechnics, and Community Colleges — symbolising the collective voice of higher education students across Malaysia.

Through transparent and constructive dialogue, six critical resolutions were presented, addressing graduate employability, cost of living, digitalisation of education, mental well-being, leadership development, and global mobility. These discussions contribute meaningfully to the ongoing development of the Rancangan Pendidikan Tinggi Malaysia (RPTM) and the broader direction of Malaysia’s higher education agenda.

The MMU SRC delegation, headed by President Darren John, represented MMU at the national level and actively contributed to these important conversations.

We extend our sincere appreciation to the Minister of Higher Education, Zambry Abd Kadir @zambryabdkadir , and the Deputy Minister of Higher Education, Adam Adli Abd Halim @adamadli_ , as well as the Ministry of Higher Education (MOHE) @mohe.official , Majlis Perundingan Pelajar Kebangsaan (MPPK) @officialmppk , and Yayasan Perkasa Siswa @perkasasiswa for providing this meaningful platform for student voices to be heard at the national level.

MMU SRC remains firmly committed to nation-building and to representing the voices of MMU students confidently and responsibly in shaping Malaysia’s future.

Sharing the Spirit of Ramadan🌙✨

On 10th March, SRC MMU distributed 100 packs of free food to students and staff for iftar in celebration of the holy month of Ramadan. 

Through this initiative, we hope to share the spirit of generosity, unity, and compassion that Ramadan represents within our campus community.

We would also like to express our appreciation to the Ministry of Higher Education @mohe.official for their support in making initiatives like this possible.

SRC MMU wishes everyone Selamat Hari Raya Aidilfitri. May this festive season bring joy, peace, and meaningful moments with your loved ones.

Let us continue to uplift one another and remember that even the smallest act of kindness can create a meaningful impact. ✨

Best regards,
Students’ Representative Council
2025/2026

As a supporting partner, we’re excited to share the final call for applications for Startup World Cup Malaysia 2026 🚀

Startup World Cup is a global startup competition that brings together top startups from around the world. The 2 Malaysia champions will represent the country at the Grand Finale in Silicon Valley, competing for a USD 1,000,000 investment prize.
Selected startups will also gain access to:
• A Startup Bootcamp focused on pitch readiness and global scaling
• Founder upskilling sessions with ecosystem leaders and investors
• Opportunities to connect with venture capitalists, corporates, and international partners

If you are building a startup and ready to scale beyond Malaysia, we encourage you to apply.
📅 Application deadline: 20 March 2026

🔗 Apply here: bit.ly/swcmalaysia2026

MMU SRC President at Majlis Perundingan Pelajar Kebangsaan (MPPK) Elections 2026/2027 🇲🇾

MMU SRC President Darren John participated in the Majlis Perundingan Pelajar Kebangsaan (MPPK) Elections for the 2026/2027 term, held from 12–14 February 2026. The platform serves as an important national forum that brings together student representatives from across Malaysia to strengthen and elevate the collective student voice at the national level, supported by the Kementerian Pendidikan Tinggi (KPT).

We would like to congratulate President Darren John on his appointment as Eksekutif Media dan Komunikasi Korporat, reflecting continued leadership and active contribution within the national student ecosystem.

We also extend our sincere congratulations to Muhammad Irfan Firdaus Bin Md Fauzi, YDP MPP Universiti Teknologi Malaysia (UTM), on being elected as President of MPPK for the 2026/2027 term.

MMU SRC remains committed to strengthening collaboration between IPTA and IPTS institutions, fostering constructive engagement, and working collectively for the betterment of students and the nation.

We wish the newly elected MPPK leadership a productive and impactful term ahead.

Built Together, Achieved Together ✨

50 achievements.

One term.

Countless memories and milestones along the way.

Throughout the 2024/2025 term, SRC worked together to plan, organise, and deliver initiatives for the student community. 

Each achievement reflects the teamwork, dedication, and effort behind the scenes 🤝

Thank you to everyone who supported, participated, and believed in our journey 💙

As we close this chapter, we wish the next SRC term all the best🌟

Yours Truly,
Students’ Representative Council 2024/2025
